author: fleece
text:
Terminology sweep: tracker / workspace

Per SPEC §3 align docs and code on tracker / workspace
terminology. "Tracker" is the git repo hosting the shared
balls/tasks branch; "workspace" is the repo where bl runs
(the recommended deployment, the README's old "bare central
hub", is now "bare workspace"). "External tracker" is
reserved for Jira / Linear / GitHub Issues.

Touches user-facing prose (README, SKILL.md), the SPEC docs
where terminology now diverged from the latest SPEC-tracker-
state.md, CLI help (HUBDIR -> WORKSPACE_DIR, "Initialized bare
balls hub" -> "... workspace"), Rust doc comments, an
internal helper rename (bootstrap_bare_hub ->
bootstrap_bare_workspace; Store::init_bare param renamed), and
the state_repo test helpers (hub_repo / hub_url / empty_hub ->
tracker_repo / tracker_url / empty_tracker).

Deliberately left alone: the master_url / state_remote serde
fields and the .balls/master.json literal path (migration
anchors per SPEC §5/§12); the legacy-migration test fixtures in
tracker_address_tests.rs and init_safety.rs that reference a
git remote literally named "hub"; and the looks_like_url
URL-parser fixtures. SPEC §4 still describes "standalone" and
"federated" as the two configurations of the unified
model, so those words remain where they describe configuration
rather than a mode.

Supersedes bl-7cea (document master_url) — master_url
retired. SPEC §16 conformance suite passes; make check
(clippy -D warnings, line-length, 100% coverage) clean.
